title: aster shortii subsp. azureus
intro: Aster shortii subsp. azureus, also known as azure blue aster, is a flowering plant species within the Asteraceae family, native to the southeastern United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help treat various health issues, including wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. The medicinal properties of azure blue aster can be attributed to its rich content of flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds, offering several health benefits such as reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Aster shortii subsp. azureus are typically dried and used as a tea, tincture, or infused in oil for topical application.
